ideas.name: learnbridge
ideas.problem: Children studying alone after school (ages 6–9, CBSE 5th) struggle silently when they get stuck on concepts like algebra. Parents are unavailable during study hours — not unwilling. Kids lose interest, pretend to understand, or get sent to tuition which feels like more school and breeds resistance.
ideas.solution: A web app that teaches CBSE 5th grade algebra through illustrated step-by-step walkthroughs + quizzes. It detects when a child is stuck using time-on-task and consecutive wrong answers, gives the child a 1-minute nudge timer first, then alerts the parent via WhatsApp if the child still does not progress. The parent receives guided prompts to support — not teach — the child through the specific stuck point.
ideas.target_users: Primary: Children (CBSE 5th grade, ~10–11 years) studying independently on a browser. Secondary: Working parents who want to help but are unavailable to monitor — reached via WhatsApp alerts.

tech_stack.infra: Vercel (web) + Supabase (auth + db + realtime) + WhatsApp Business API (parent alerts)

mvp_features.title: Illustrated Step-by-Step Walkthroughs
mvp_features.description: Static illustrated walkthroughs covering CBSE 5th grade algebra topics. Each concept broken into steps with illustrations. No video production required.

mvp_features.title: Adaptive Quiz Engine
mvp_features.description: Quizzes after each walkthrough to test understanding. Tracks correct/incorrect streaks per session.

mvp_features.title: Adaptive Quiz Engine + Stuck Detection
mvp_features.description: 4-level question system per subsection (1=easiest, 4=hardest). Child starts at L3. Correct → advances to L4 → passes stage. Wrong → shown explanation + retry at same level. Parent is nudged only when child exhausts all retry paths (fails L3 retry OR fails L4 retry). Stuck detection is embedded in level progression — no time-based thresholds.

mvp_features.title: Parent WhatsApp Alert
mvp_features.description: WhatsApp message to parent when child remains stuck after the nudge timer. Message includes topic name and stuck signal that triggered it.

mvp_features.title: Parent Guided Prompt (via WhatsApp)
mvp_features.description: WhatsApp message with a simple coaching script — what to say/ask the child to help them through the stuck point. Not full teaching, just targeted prompts.

mvp_features.title: Progress Dashboard
mvp_features.description: Web dashboard showing completed sessions, quiz scores over time, and which topics triggered alerts. Accessible to both child and parent using the shared account. Link to dashboard is included in WhatsApp stuck alerts.

mvp_features.title: Subscription + Auth
mvp_features.description: Monthly subscription. One shared account per family (child and parent use the same login). Signup captures parent email + WhatsApp number. No separate parent account.

success_metrics: % of sessions completed without parent alert
Quiz score improvement per topic across attempts
Parent WhatsApp response rate and response time
Average sessions per week per child
ideas.constraints: Solo founder building MVP with AI agents. Web for child (browser), WhatsApp for parent alerts. CBSE 5th grade algebra as the only subject at launch. No team, no external budget — lean and fast.

ideas.name: veettukkar
ideas.problem: Homeowners in Kerala struggle to find reliable daily-wage workers — coconut tree climbers, house painters, cleaners, cement construction workers, plumbers, and electricians — on short notice. Workers depend entirely on word-of-mouth and personal networks, limiting their reach and leaving them with unpredictable income. Both sides lose: homeowners can’t get help when they need it; workers miss jobs they could have taken.
ideas.solution: A lightweight mobile app connecting Kerala homeowners with verified local daily-wage workers. Homeowners post a job (skill type, date, half-day or full-day, location). Nearby available workers are notified and can accept. A ratings system builds trust on both sides. No payment processing in MVP — workers are paid in cash as they always have been. The app handles matching and reliability.
ideas.target_users: Primary: Homeowners in Kerala who occasionally need skilled or semi-skilled household work — one-time jobs like climbing a coconut tree, painting a room, or calling a plumber for a leaking pipe. Secondary: Daily-wage workers (coconut climbers, painters, cleaners, construction workers, plumbers, electricians) who want a steady stream of local job opportunities beyond their personal network.

mvp_features.title: Worker Profile
mvp_features.description: Workers register with name, phone, skills (one or more of the 6 categories), location (district/area), day rate and half-day rate, and availability. Profile photo optional.

mvp_features.title: Job Posting by Homeowner
mvp_features.description: Homeowner posts a job: skill category, date, duration (half-day or full-day), location, and a short description. Visible to nearby workers only.

mvp_features.title: Nearby Worker Matching
mvp_features.description: Show available workers within ~10km for the requested skill and date. Workers are ranked by rating and distance.

mvp_features.title: Job Notification & Accept/Decline
mvp_features.description: Worker receives push notification when a matching job is posted. Accepts or declines. Homeowner is notified of acceptance and gets worker’s contact number to confirm details.

mvp_features.title: Post-Job Ratings
mvp_features.description: After job completion, homeowner rates the worker (1–5 stars + optional comment). Worker can rate the homeowner. Ratings are visible on both profiles.

mvp_features.title: Malayalam-First UI
mvp_features.description: All UI text in Malayalam by default. English as secondary option. Critical for adoption by workers who may not be fluent in English.

mvp_features.title: Phone OTP Authentication
mvp_features.description: Register and log in with phone number + SMS OTP only. No email required. Handles workers on low-end Android phones with limited literacy.

success_metrics: % of posted jobs filled within 24 hours
Worker repeat job rate (jobs completed per worker per month)
Homeowner repeat posting rate (jobs posted per month per user)
Day-30 app retention for both user types
Average worker rating across completed jobs
ideas.constraints: Solo founder, lean budget, Kerala-only MVP. Workers likely use low-end Android phones — app must be lightweight and work on 3G/4G. Malayalam is primary language. No payment processing in v1 (cash model). No team.

direction.reasoning: Directly mirrors how the problem is experienced: homeowner posts a job, nearby workers are notified and accept. Real-time notifications drive engagement on both sides. Ratings accumulate naturally. Proven marketplace model (analogues: Urban Company in India, TaskRabbit globally). Incorporates an 'available today' toggle from Direction 6 to handle urgency within the same product.

feedback.problem_clarity: Sharp and specific. Two identifiable user groups with concrete friction. Six worker categories eliminate ambiguity. Cold-start strategy not addressed in spec.
feedback.market_viability: Real market, real gap vs. Urban Company (which doesn't serve informal Kerala labour market). Monetisation deferred to v2 — acceptable for MVP. WhatsApp groups are the real competition.
feedback.tech_feasibility: React Native + Firebase is fast and proven. Phone OTP via Firebase is trivial. Malayalam localisation adds frontend work but is necessary. Low-end Android compatibility requires discipline.
feedback.user_validation: Problem is experienced daily — no education needed. Workers motivated by income. Homeowners have urgent latent demand. No user interviews yet; first-worker onboarding may need to be in-person.

refined_mvp_features.title: Worker Profile
refined_mvp_features.description: Workers register with name, phone, skills (one or more of 6 categories), location, day/half-day rate. Availability calendar. Optional photo.

refined_mvp_features.title: Available Today Toggle
refined_mvp_features.description: Workers flag themselves as available same-day. Surfaces them at top of relevant matches for urgent requests.

refined_mvp_features.title: Job Posting by Homeowner
refined_mvp_features.description: Homeowner posts job: skill category, date, duration (half/full day), location, description. Optional 'urgent' tag for same-day needs.

refined_mvp_features.title: Nearby Worker Matching
refined_mvp_features.description: Show available workers within ~10km for skill and date. Ranked by rating, then availability (today > later), then distance.

refined_mvp_features.title: Job Notification & Accept/Decline
refined_mvp_features.description: Worker gets push notification for matching jobs. Accepts or declines. Homeowner gets worker phone number on acceptance.

refined_mvp_features.title: Post-Job Ratings
refined_mvp_features.description: Homeowner rates worker (1–5 stars + comment). Worker rates homeowner. Visible on profiles. This is the key differentiator vs. WhatsApp groups.

refined_mvp_features.title: Malayalam-First UI
refined_mvp_features.description: All text in Malayalam by default. English toggle. Essential for worker adoption.

refined_mvp_features.title: Phone OTP Authentication
refined_mvp_features.description: Phone number + SMS OTP only. No email. Works on low-end Android with limited literacy.

key_risks: Cold-start: need strategy to onboard first 30 workers (likely in-person in one neighbourhood)
Geographic density: sparse areas may not have enough workers per skill
WhatsApp competition: ratings/trust must be made prominently valuable
review.pilot_recommendation: Start in one district (Ernakulam or Thiruvananthapuram) before expanding

tech_stack.infra: Firebase (Auth + Firestore + Functions + Storage + FCM) + Twilio WhatsApp API + DigiLocker API
other: Cloud Functions v2 (Node.js 20) for job matching and notifications
Firebase Storage for worker profile photos
